The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a #Message to another person. 

<ex><fex name="Communicator">Her father</fex> never <t>mentioned</t> it.</ex>

The #Addressee receives a #Message from the #Communicator. 

<ex>I <t>mentioned</t> it <fex name="Addressee">to him</fex> in direct terms.</ex>

A proposition or set of propositions that the #Communicator wants the #Addressee to believe or take for granted. 

<ex>A CBS report did <t>mention</t> <fex name="Message">that the First Lady was required to wear a flak jacket for her safety</fex>.</ex>

The physical or abstract setting in which the #Message is conveyed. 

<ex><fex name="Medium">The report</fex> also <t>mentioned</t> contamination problems during the manufacture.</ex>
The #Depictive describes the state of the #Communicator.

The #Manner in which the #Communicator communicates.

<ex>Her mother <t>mentioned</t> <fex name="Manner">casually</fex> that Parr had gone to Belfast.</ex>
The #Means by which the #Communicator communicates.
When the the #Message is mentioned.
#Duration is the length of time during which the communication takes place.

The number of times the #Message is mentioned by the #Communicator.

<ex>I <t>mentioned</t> it <fex name="Iterations">fourteen times</fex> and it still wasn t enough.</ex>

Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
How often the #Communicator mentions the #Message.

The relevant entity or state-of-affairs that the #Communicator mentions.

<ex>If someone <t>mentions</t> <fex name="Specified_content">things like ration books</fex> , my stomach turns.</ex>

It denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
The state of the world when the #Communicator mentions the Message or #Specified_content.
The location where the #Communicator communicates the #Message.
The purpose for which the #Communicator mentions the #Specified_content.
The #Force is the body of soldiers that acts in military capacity for the #Possessor. This FE is incorporated in each word in this frame.

The #Possessor is the nation, institution, or individual who possesses and/or controls the #Force.

<ex>The American invasion, which drew 26000 American troops against <fex name="pos">General Noriega s</fex> <t>army</t>, was the largest US military operation since Vietnam.</ex>
<ex>Unfortunately, <fex name="pos">They</fex> don t <m>have</m> a very strong <t>army</t>.</ex>

A characterization of the #Force.

<ex>And while Serbia is keen to invest in its <fex name="des">dilapidated</fex> <t>armed</t> <t>forces</t>, Montenegro wants to introduce a 4000-strong professional army.</ex>

The consituent members of the #Force.

<ex>The US has an entirely <fex name="mem">citizen</fex> <t>army</t>.</ex>

The #Domain in which the #Force operates.

<ex>The Persians originally planned to rely solely on their <fex name="dom">land</fex> <t>army</t> to advance beyond Athens.</ex>
<ex>You seem to think that our intelligence and <fex name="dom">covert ops</fex> <t>forces</t> and our military are only able to conduct one mission at a time.</ex>

A (permanent or temporary) #Goal of the #Force.

<ex>Right at this time of emergency, the <fex name="goa">revolutionary</fex> <t>army</t> made one attack after another in their rear, thus adding to their confusion.</ex>
The length of time that the particular military organization has existed.

The #Force carries out the operation. Depending on the size of the operation, this may be as few as one individual or as large as the armed forces of a nation.

<ex><fex name="mil">He</fex> died while <m>carrying out</m> counter-IED <t>operations</t>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="mil">US forces</fex> currently <t>operating</t> in Iraq are due to be recalled within the next several months.</ex>
The #Possessor controls the army and ultimately directs its goals and methods.
The #Area in which the #Military is conducting operations.

The nation, group, or situation which the operation opposes.

<ex><t>Operations</t> <fex name="opp">against Afghanistan</fex> continue to be carried out.</ex>
The #Time at which the operations are carried out.
The length of time over which the operation is carried out.

A description of the operation or participants that are not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Military that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
The means by which the #Military carries out its operation.

The #Domain of the operation, e.g., military, intelligence.

<ex>NATO forces are <m>conducting</m> <fex name="dom">military</fex> <t>operations</t> in highly volatile regions.</ex>

The #Goal of the operation.

<ex>He was captured in a covert <fex name="goa">arrest</fex> <t>operation</t> carried out by an elite group of highly-trained Marines.</ex>

The location of the operation.

<ex>This sort of <t>operation</t> should only be <m>conducted</m> <fex name="pla">in open areas</fex>.</ex>
The #Miner searches for mineral #Resources.
The #Resource is usually buried in the ground, and is searched for by the #Miner.
The location in which the #Miner searches for the #Resource
The means by which the #Miner uses to obtain the #Resource.
The success or failure of the attempt to obtain the #Resource by the #Miner.
The time at which the #Miner obtains the #Resource.
This is the person whose #Misdeed is being judged with respect to its morality.
The assessment of the #Wrongdoer is based on his or her #Misdeed.
The #Severity is the degree to which the #Misdeed offends the moral sense of the #Viewpoint.
The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
The time at which a #Misdeed is performed.
The location where a #Misdeed occurs.
The state-of-affairs that the #Wrongdoer is hoping to accomplish through their #Misdeed.
An action taken by the #Wrongdoer which accomplishes the #Misdeed.

Any description of the #Misdeed which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a #Wrongdoer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
An individual who is harmed by the #Misdeed of the #Wrongdoer.
How often the #Wrongdoer commits the #Misdeed.
The #Money is used as a medium of exchange.
The use for which the #Money is intended or designed.
The government (or possibly other organization) that mints, issues and guarantees the legal use of the #Money.
The time at which an #Money comes into existence or begins to be circulated.
The term used to refer to the #Money.
An indication of the subtype of #Money.
Any indication of what makes up the #Money, including components, ingredients, etc.
The process or means by which the #Money comes to be possessed.
The #Possessor has control over the use the #Money.

A particular dedicated plan that the #Possessor has for how to spend the #Money.

<ex>I don't have <t>money</t> <fex name="Use">for clothes</fex> .</ex>

The assessment of the #Evaluee is based on his or her #Behavior.

The #Theme is the entity that changes location. Note that it is not necessarily a self-mover.
<ex>The explosion made <fex name="Thm">me</fex> <t>move</t> in a hurry.</ex>

The #Source is the location the #Theme occupies initially before its change of location.

<ex>The policeman <t>moved</t> <fex name="Src">away from the door</fex>.</ex>

The #Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme travels  or to a landmark by which the #Theme travels.

<ex>Jo <t>moved</t> <fex name="Path">past Dad</fex> into the hall.</ex>

The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in.

<ex>The car <t>moved</t> <fex name="Goal">into the slow lane</fex>.</ex>

#Area identifies the setting in which the #Theme s movement takes place without a specified #Path.

<ex>Emily <t>moved</t> restlessly <fex name="Area">around the room</fex>.</ex>

#Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of the Motion. 
<ex>The twig <t>floated</t> atop the water <fex name="Dist">for about 100 yards</fex>.</ex>

This FE is used for the #Speed at which the #Theme moves.

<ex>The ash cloud <t>drifted</t> <fex name="Spd">at twenty-five kilometres per hour</fex>.</ex>

The #Carrier is the means of conveyance of the #Theme.

<ex>The nest <t>blew</t> from its tree <fex name="Car">in a gale</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Duration of  time for which the Motion takes place.

<ex>The balloon <t>floated</t> <fex name="Dur">for  hours</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Time when the Motion occurs.

<ex>A radioactive cloud <t>drifted</t> across Britain <fex name="Time">after the fire at the Windscale nuclear factory</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Manner in which the Motion takes place.
<ex>A naval airship <t>drifted</t> <fex name="Manr">wildy</fex> in the stormy sea.</ex>

#Result identifies the ultimate effect of the Motion.
<ex>The feathers <t>floated</t> <fex name="Result">together</fex> on the water s surface.</ex>
This FE is used for any #Depictive phrase describing the state of the #Theme while the motion is occuring.
The #Place is the general area in which a specific motion (with #Source, #Path, or #Goal) occurs.

The extent to which the #Theme cross a boundary as it moves away from a #Source region or into a #Goal region.

<ex>Pluto has <t>moved</t> <fex name="Degree">fully</fex>into Sagittarius</ex>

This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the #Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body.  Often #Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Protagonist, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.

In cases wherein the #Theme is construed as a sentient entity, this FE denotes the state of affairs that the entity wishes to bring about by moving.

<ex>Johnny <t>went</t> to Langley  <fex name="pur">to turn himself in</fex>.</ex>

The shape of the overall #Path travelled by the #Theme.

<ex>They <t>drifted</t> <fex name="sha">in circles</fex> for weeks before rescue .</ex>
An event that the described motion forms an integral part of.
This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.

The frame element <fex name="Iterations">Iterations</fex> is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

This FE identifies the general #Area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.

<ex>Soot-contaminated snow <t>falls</t> <fex name="Area">in Slovakia s mountainous regions</fex>.</ex>
This FE identifies any #Depictive phrase describing the the state of the #Theme during the motion.

#Distance identifies a phrase that characterizes the extent of motion of the #Theme.

<ex>The student <t>plunged</t> from a road bridge <fex name="Dist">more than 100 feet</fex> to his death.</ex>

#Duration is  used for the length of time for which the Motion takes place.

<ex>In one storm , 46 inches of rain <t>fell</t> <fex name="Dur">in 24 hours</fex> .</ex>

The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in (often unintentionally).

<ex>The rivals both <t>fell</t> backwards <fex name="Goal">into empty space</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Manner in which the Motion takes place.

<ex>A few heavy spots of rain <t>fell</t> on the pavement <fex name="Mnr">like coins</fex>.</ex>

#Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme travels  or to a landmark past which the #Theme travels.

<ex>The boys <t>plummeted</t> <fex name="Path">down the stairs</fex> into the basement.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Speed at which the motion occurs.

<ex>Michelle <t>toppled</t> <fex name="Spd">slowly</fex> to the floor.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Time when the Motion occurs.

<ex><fex name="Time">In 1977</fex> Nigel <t>plunged</t> to his death in his car on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>

The #Carrier is the means of conveyance of the #Theme.

<ex>Nigel <t>plunged</t> to his death <fex name="Car">in his car</fex> on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>

The #Theme is the entity that changes location.

<ex>In 1977, <fex name="Thm"> Nigel</fex> <t>plunged</t> to his death in his car on a mountain road in southern France.</ex>
The #Source is the location the #Theme occupies before the Motion occurs.  <ex>The man <t>toppled</t> <fex name="Src">from the passenger seat</fex> into the road.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Result of the motion. 

<ex>Marie shoved Simon, and he <t>toppled</t> <fex name="Result">over</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Place where motion specified by #Source, #Path or #Goal occurs.
<ex><fex name="Place">In the kitchen</fex>, the towel <t>dropped</t> onto the floor.</ex>

The extent to which the #Theme crosses boundary as it enters a #Goal region or exits a #Source region.

<ex>Sucking in a deep breath, he <t>plunged</t> <fex name="Degree">fully</fex> into the cold water</ex>

This FE is used for expressing some part of the #Theme participant that makes contact with a horizontal surface. 

<ex>With a loud thump, the monster <t>fell</t> <fex name="poi">on its back</fex>, the earth trembling as he hit the ground.</ex>
The shape formed by the path followed by the #Theme.
An event whereby the motion of the #Theme comes about.

The direction of motion of the #Theme.  #Direction characterizes the #Path of motion according to some system of orientation, most often that imposed by gravity.

<ex>I hope your egg does n t <t>fall</t> <fex name="dir">down</fex> .</ex>
Any state of affairs or event from which the particular direction motion logically follows.
The state of affairs that the agentive #Theme hopes to bring about by moving in a particular direction.
The state of the world as the action occurs.
The #Cause describes the reason for the motion. <ex>The glasses <t>clinked</t> across the table <fex name="Cause">during the tornado</fex>.</ex>

The #Theme is the entity that changes location.  In sentences containing a #Cause, the #Theme is typically the NP Object.  In other sentences, the #Theme is normally the External Argument.


<ex><fex name="Theme">Pat</fex> <t>crunched</t> over the fresh snow.</ex>

The #Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of Motion.

<ex>The train <t>rumbled</t> <fex name="Source">out of the station</fex>.</ex>

The #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal.

<ex>The truck <t>roared</t> <fex name="Path">through the tunnel</fex>.</ex>

The #Goal is any expression which tells where the #Theme ends up as a result of the Motion.

<ex>The plates <t>clattered</t> <fex name="Goal">to the floor</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the general #Area in which motion takes place, used particularly if the motion is understood as following a complex or non-linear path.

<ex>The fly <t>buzzed</t> <fex name="Area">about the room</fex>.</ex>

The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of Motion.  This frame element occurs very infrequently with Motion_noise verbs.

<ex>The car <t>wheezed</t> <fex name="Dist">the two miles</fex> into town.</ex>

The #Speed is the rate at which the motion occurs.

<ex>My Saab <t>purrs</t> along easily <fex name="Speed">at 80 miles an hour</fex>.</ex>

The #Depictive indicates the state of the #Theme during Motion.

<ex>The car <t>chugged</t> across the ruts, <fex name="Dep">unstoppable</fex>.</ex>

The #Manner is the way in which the Motion takes place.

<ex>The drone <t>buzzed</t> <fex name="Manr">curiously</fex> across the room and vanished behind the table.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Means by which the #Theme moves in the specified way.

<ex>The autochopper <t>whirred</t> along<fex name="Mns"> by supplementing its onboard power with solar generators</fex>.</ex>

The #Result is the ultimate effect of the Motion.

<ex>The door of the empty box <t>creaked</t> <fex name="Result">open</fex> in the wind.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Degree to which the Motion occurs.

<ex>Suddenly , the pole <t>thumped</t> <fex name="Degr">really hard</fex> into an unseen object.</ex>
The #Place describes the location of a more specific motion (which has #Source, #Path, #Goal, or #Area).

The period of time for which the #Theme moves under accompaniment of noise.

<ex>From there they <t>roared</t> northward <fex name="Duration">for about 10 minutes</fex> before turning up the beach approach onto A1.</ex>
The #Time when the motion event takes place.  <ex>The train <t>screeched</t> into the station <fex name="Time">at half past seven</fex></ex>
The amount of time for the movement is ongoing.

This FE identifies the #Time when the motion occurs.

<ex>The truck <t>moved</t> <fex name="Time">at 10 o clock</fex>.</ex>

The #Area is the setting in which the #Theme s movement takes place. 

<ex>Emily rose to her feet and <t>moved</t> restlessly <fex name="Area">around the room</fex>.</ex>

The #Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.

<ex>Pat <t>accompanied</t> <fex name="Cotheme">me</fex> down the street.</ex>
<ex>The squirrel <t>chased</t> <fex name="Cotheme">after the nut</fex>.</ex>
The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.

The #Goal is the location the #Theme ends up in.

<ex>The car accelerated and <t>moved</t> <fex name="Goal">into the slow lane</fex>, as he passed.</ex>

The #Path refers to (a part of the) ground that the #Theme travels over or to a landmark the #Theme travels past.

<ex>The door opened , and he <t>moved</t> <fex name="Path">past Dad</fex>, into the hall.</ex>

The #Source is the location the #Theme initially occupies before its change of location.

<ex>The policeman <t>moved</t> <fex name="Source">away from the door</fex>.</ex>

The #Speed is the rate at which motion occurs.

<ex>The car <t>moved</t> along <fex name="Spd">at 55 miles per hour</fex>.</ex>

The #Theme is the entity that changes location. 

<ex>The explosion made <fex name="Thm">me</fex> <t>move</t> in a hurry.</ex>

The #Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the #Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the #Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with  in  or  by .
A player or team that performs the technical-tactical actions.
The kind of move.
Action or move taken by an #Athlete, according to the rules of the sport.
A physical entity that is participating in non-translational motion.

The point or set of points that define the limits of motion for the #Theme.  For spinning motions, it is the axis, for vibrating it is a boundary, for swinging it is a point.
<ex>Balls <t>spin</t> <fex name="Fix_loc">on their axes </fex>.</ex>
<ex>The window <t>vibrated</t> <fex name="Fix_loc">in the frame</fex>.</ex>

The number of times the #Theme returns to a state in a given duration.
<ex>They <t>spun</t> it <fex name="Per">at 1000 rpms</fex>.</ex>
The amount of rotation that the #Theme undergoes.
The time at which the #Theme is in bounded motion.
The location where the bounded motion happens.

The direction of rotation of the #Theme.
<ex>The dial <t>rotated</t> <fex name="Dir">clockwise</fex> one revolution, then the vault opened.</ex>
The #Manner describes the way in which the Theme moves in place.
The Result describes the end state brought about by the motion in place.

The Cause_external describes the force which sets into motion the motion.
<ex>The strings <t>vibrated</t> <fex name="Explanation">with the impact of the wind</fex>.</ex>
A description of the #Theme during the action.
The state of affairs that the agentive Theme hopes to bring about by moving.
The shape formed by the path that the #Theme follows.
An artifact made or adpted to produce musical sounds.

While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion, or it backgrounds motion entirely.

The #Agent is the one who passes the #Theme.  The #Agent is generally realized as the External Argument.

<ex><fex name="Agent">Pat</fex> <t>passed</t> me the ball.</ex>

The FE #Theme is generally an NP Object that is passed to the #Recipient.

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> <fex name="Theme">the ball</fex> to the goalie.</ex>

The FE #Source is the starting point of the passing action

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Source">from behind the defensemen</fex>.</ex>

The FE #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor #Goal.

<ex>I <t>passed</t> him the ball <fex name="Path">over the wall</fex>.</ex>

The FE #Recipient is the entity which possesses the #Theme as a result of the passing.

<ex>Kim <t>passed</t> the salt <fex name="Recipient">to her mother</fex>.</ex>

The FE #Distance is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the #Theme.

<ex>Pat <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Distance">50 meters</fex> to me.</ex>

The FE #Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which passing takes place when the passing is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element.

<ex>The team <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Area">around the court</fex>.</ex>

The state of a participant during the passing action.
<ex>We <t>passed</t> the ball around, <fex name="Depictive">gleeful</fex>.</ex>
The FE #Means indicates the action that is taken that results in the motion of the #Theme

The FE #Manner is any expression which describes a property of the motion of the #Theme which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as #Manner expressions.

An entity saliently involved in the transmission of force from an #Agent to a #Theme.  It may be the #Agent s bodypart, or some kind of device.

<ex>When playing basketball, you should <t>pass</t> <fex name="Ins">with both hands</fex>.</ex>

The #Place is the general area that provides a setting for a specific motion (with #Source, #Path, or #Goal).

<ex>The players <t>passed</t> the ball to each other <fex name="Place">at the park</fex>.</ex>
The time at which the passing of the #Theme occurs.

A resultant state that the #Theme is in at the end of its motion.


<ex>The center <t>passed</t> the ball <fex name="Result">clear of the double-team</fex>.</ex>

A previous state that the #Theme was in before the beginning of its motion.

<ex>Paul <t>passed</t> the ball away <fex name="Initial_state">from the danger of the double-team</fex>.</ex>
This FE denotes a proposition from which the main clause logically follows.

The #Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that the #Item is a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>Most recently, <fex name="agt">Grant Palmer</fex> has <t>palmed</t> himself <t>off</t> as a high-placed "insider" in
the Church.</ex>

Any description of the passing off event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>).
An intentional action performed by the #Agent that contributes to the #Item being perceived as a (particular kind of) #Simulated_entity.

The #Item is the concrete or abstract entity that the Agent misleadingly represents as a particular (kind of) #Entity.

<ex>From all reports Muhammad <t>passed</t> <fex name="Item">the child</fex> <t>off</t> as his son and behaved accordingly.</ex>
An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by passing the #Item off as a (particular kind of) #Simulated_entity.
The #Explanation denotes a proposition from which the passing-off event logically follows.

The #Simulated_entity is a particular individual or a token of a particular type that the #Item is represented as.

<ex>The colleague who stole your idea and <t>passed</t> it <t>off</t> <fex name="Simulated_entity">as her own</fex>? </ex>

The #Place is the location that is the setting for the passing off event.

<ex><fex name="Place">In London</fex> she <t>passed</t> herself <t>off</t> as a rich American heiress.</ex>

The #Audience denotes the people that the #Agent intends to mislead into thinking that the #Item is a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>Ritchie <t>passed</t> it <t>off</t> <fex name="Audience">to them</fex> as his own original thinking.</ex>
The time at which the #Agent gives the impression that the #Item is the #Simulated_entity.

<fex name="Road">The bike trail</fex> <t>zigzagged</t> through the woods.

Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of the kind of motion a hypothetical mover would have (including steadiness, grace, and general comparisons) count as #Manner expressions.  

<ex>Drifting clouds lent a mystical touch while the narrow road <t>twisted</t> <fex name="Manner">crazily</fex> through ancient stone villages on the valley floor.</ex>

Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element #Distance. 

<ex>The thin line of dirt and gravel <t>wound</t> <fex name="Distance">another ten miles</fex> and on into the Ozarks before it petered out completely.</ex>

This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path.  

<ex>The paths <t>crisscrossed</t> <fex name="Area">the desert</fex>.</ex>

Note that this FE should not be used for any locational modifier which would occur just as naturally with a non-motion predicate, like speech.

Extent to which the described configuration deviates from the norm for a #Road.

<ex>The road <t>bent</t> <fex name="Degree">slightly</fex> before resuming its long, straight march to the sea .</ex>

An state of affairs involving the #Road that allows the #Road to be in the specified configuration.

<ex>As the Condell Road the route <t>skirts</t> the northside of Limerick <fex name="Means">by passing through Westfield s bird sanctuary on the north bank of the River Shannon</fex>.</ex>
Result of an event

The #Speed is the rate at which an imaginary traveler on the #Road would move.

<ex>The road <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> <t>drops</t> after passing the final turn.</ex>

Depictive phrase describing the character of the #Road.

<ex>The trail <t>snaked</t> down the mountainside, <fex name="Dep-Und">arduous and nearly impassable</fex>.</ex>

The #Place is where the motion (which is specified by a #Source, #Path or #Goal) is located.

<ex><fex name="Place">In the mountains</fex>, Highway 60 <t>winds</t> and swerves a lot, so we lost a couple of hours from my estimate.</ex>

The configuration formed by the entire #Path along the #Road.
<ex>The cavern <t>descended</t> <fex name="PS">in a spiral</fex>.</ex>

The direction in which a "fictive" mover would travel along a #Road.

<ex>The pass <t>dips</t> <fex name="dir">down</fex> about 2000 feet, and then <fex name="dir">back up</fex> again.</ex>

The time when the fictive motion takes place.  This may describe the time when the #Road or a particular piece of the #Road existed, or it may implicitly describe the time when an actual motion event occurred on the #Road.

<ex><fex name="tim">Thousands of years ago</fex>, the Roman roads <t>criss-crossed</t> this landscape.</ex>
<ex><fex name="tim">After it reached the edge of the forest</fex>, the road <t>slanted</t> upwards and grew rough.</ex>

The state-of-affairs that the #Road brings about by being in a particular configuration.

<ex>The road<t>wound</t> around <fex name="Purp">in order to circumvent the house.</fex>.</ex>

The series of interconnected locations that the #Theme traverses.

<ex>In insects, blood is confined to vessels during only a portion of its <fex name="pat"><t>circuit</t></fex> through the body.</ex>

The #Source is one of the #Endpoints treated as a source.

<ex>The Cassini spacecraft will travel 3.5 billion kilometers (2.175 billion miles) along its <t>path</t> <fex name="Src">from Earth</fex> to Saturn.</ex>

The #Goal is the endpoint of the #Path.

<ex>The Cassini spacecraft will travel 3.5 billion kilometers (2.175 billion miles) along its <t>path</t> from Earth <fex name="Goal">to Saturn</fex>.</ex>

The #Endpoints are two (or more) named endpoints of the #Path.

<ex>This Pacific typhoon traveled far inland, knocking down everything in its <t>path</t> <fex name="Endpoints">between Los Angeles and Denver</fex>.</ex>

The #Characteristic is a characteristic(s) of the #Path.

<ex>Unlike Hubble, Compton was not designed to be serviced by astronauts in its <fex name="Char">270
mile</fex> <t>orbit</t>.</ex>

An entity relative to which the #Theme moves on its #Path .


<ex>Lenny killed at least nine people and caused millions of dollars in damage on
its <t>course</t> <fex name="Landmark">past 16 Caribbean islands</fex>.</ex>

The #Direction is the direction or directions the #Path runs.

<ex>There was huge relief that Hurricane Emily s projected <fex name="Dir">westward</fex>
<t>path</t> across the Caribbean is likely to track to the south of oil and gas fields.</ex>

A setting location through which the #Path goes.

<ex>Hurrican Dennis was blamed for five deaths in Florida and Georgia -- but was much more
brutal in its <t>path</t> <fex name="gro">across the Caribbean</fex>.</ex>

The #Length is the distance the #Path extends.

<ex>The bolt traveled smoothly in its <fex name="Length">short</fex> <t>path</t> through the air to Santa.</ex>

The moving object whose motion describes the #Path.

<ex>The thick white line shows <fex name="the">the army s</fex> <t>path</t> from Uzbekistan through Iran, Iraq, Palestine, and into Italy.</ex>

The person (or group) who inspects the #Ground to see that it is safe.

<ex><fex name="pat">Detective Jones</fex> <t>patrolled</t> the northern half of the building.</ex>

The area that the #Patrol inspects with to ensure its safety.

<ex>Four soldiers with submachine guns <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="gro">the room</fex>, and an officer made his rounds at frequent intervals.</ex>

An entity whose presence would impair the desirability or safety of the #Ground.

<ex>Security guards <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="unw">for intruders</fex> while the game was in progress.</ex>

This FE is used for the #Means by which the #Patrol examines  the #Ground.

<ex><t>Policing</t> your neighborhood <fex name="mea">by walking your block and asking about new or unusual things</fex> can significantly improve your and your neighbors  safety.</ex>

The desired outcome of the patrolling. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by <ment>for</ment>, or a <ment>to</ment>-marked VP Complement.

<ex><t>Patrol</t> all unlit areas <fex name="pur">to ensure no one is hiding there</fex>.</ex>

This FE identifies the #Manner in which the #Patrol inspects the #Ground.

<ex>My officers have been <fex name="man">bravely</fex> <t>patrolling</t> that estate, on behalf of the community which we serve, and will continue to do so.</ex>

This FE identifies the extent  to which the #Patrol examines the #Ground.

<ex> This section is <fex name="deg">thoroughly</fex> <t>policed</t> by the correctional officers every morning and evening.</ex>

This FE is the position of the #Patrol during the act of perception.  Typically, it is expressed in a <ment>from</ment>-PP.

<ex>We ll have to <t>patrol</t> the roof <fex name="Location_of_protagonist">from a distance</fex>, avoiding the snipers  notice.</ex>

The desirable state of affairs which the #Patrol hopes to ensure by visiting #Ground.

<ex>All our grounds are regularly <t>patrolled</t> <fex name="des">for safety</fex>.</ex>

>The #Time when the #Patrol examines the #Ground.

<ex>Officers<t>policed</t> the parade grounds <fex name="tim">during the Memorial day events.</fex> .</ex>

The circumstances under which the #Patrol examines the #Ground.

<ex><fex name="cir">Under pressure to find something</fex>, the team <t>patrolled</t> the site for signs of illicit activity.</ex>

An entity used by the #Patrol to scrutinize the #Ground.

<ex>Scouts <t>patrolled</t> the beaches <fex name="inst">with binoculars</fex>, looking for aircraft.</ex>
Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

A description of one of the participants in the patrolling event.

<ex>They <t>patrolled</t> the parade grounds, <fex name="des">unaware of the lurking danger</fex>.</ex>

<ex>An <fex name="des">American</fex> <t>patrol</t> encounted resistance in the area.</ex>

An indication of the members of the patrol.

<ex>We <t>patrolled</t> the area <fex name="mem">with thirty soldiers</fex>.</ex>

<ex><fex name="mem">Our</fex> <t>patrol</t> was ambushed just outside the village.</ex>

The length of time of the patrol.

<ex>We ve been <t>patrolling</t> <fex name="dur">for hours</fex> and haven t even seen anyone.</ex>
The #Co-participant patrols along with the #Patrol.

The #Patrol may move around in a #Vehicle.

<ex>We typically <t>patrolled</t> this area <fex name="veh">in heavily armored cars</fex>.</ex>

The #Possessor is the organization (usually a nation or group of nations) that orchestrates the patrol.

<ex>The bombmaker was found by a <fex name="pos">NATO</fex> <t>patrol</t>.</ex>
The entities whose interrelations form the pattern.
The name of the pattern, such as "wing-T".
A descriptor of the pattern, such as "regular".
The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
It is the thing given in exchange for goods in a transaction.
In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of goods.
It is any unit in which goods or services can be measured.
Where the event takes place.
When the event occurs.

A description of the transfer not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a buyer, seller, or exchangers that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
The state-of-affairs that the buyer, seller, or exchangers wish to bring about by participating in the exchange.
The #Tourist wants the #Services and offers #Money to an #Operator in exchange for them.
The services are anything which is exchanged for money in a transaction.
The #Operator offers the #Services and exchanges them for #Money from a #Tourist.
The reason for which an event occurs.

The #Fighter has a military specialty.
The #Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the #Fighter which is concieved of as persisting over time.
The #Descriptor is a temporary condition of the #Fighter.
The #Age is the length of time the #Fighter has been alive.
The #Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the #Fighter belongs.

An expression indicating the context with which the #Fighter is associated.

<ex>Robin was the <fex name="rnk">highest-ranking</fex> <t>gunner</t> at the scene.</ex>
This FE identifies the place where the #Fighter is stationed, or primarily operates from.
The #Fighter belongs to the military of the #Loyal_side.
This FE identifies a more specific #Type of specialty that the #Fighter has.
The #Compensation is the payment that the #Fighter receives for performing military service.
The #Contract_basis describes the conditions of military membership with respect to permanency, time or duration of duty, payment basis, and so on.

The military organization to which the #Fighter belongs.

<ex>My friend is a <fex name="mil">US Navy</fex> <t><fex name="Fighter">engineer</fex></t>.</ex>
The #Origin is the place where the #Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
The #Person is the human being.
The #Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the #Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
It is a temporary condition of the #Person.
The #Age is the length of time the #Person has been alive.
The #Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the #Person belongs.
An expression indicating the context with which the #Person is associated.
#Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a #Person occupies within an organization.
This FE identifies the Place where the #Person works.
The #Employer gives compensation to an #Person for their work.
This FE identifies the #Type of the vocation that the #Person practices.
The #Compensation is the payment that the #Person receives for performing a task.
The #Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.
The FE #Perceiver refers to someone whose cognitive state or experience (e.g. knowledge, beliefs, ideas judgments or classifications) is being described.

The #Perceiver is the entity or event which causes the perceptual experience of the #Perceiver. 

<ex>So do our domestic dogs <t>perceive</t> <fex name="Sought_phenomenon">smells</fex> with the same range  of subtle `hues   as we perceive colour?</ex>

#Phenomenon is experienced by the #Perceiver. 

<ex>Kim <t>perceived</t>  <fex name="Phenomenon">a flaw</fex>.</ex>
The #Perceiver_agentive performs some action in order to have a perceptual experience.
The #Phenomenon indicates the entity or phenomenon to which the #Perceiver_agentive directs his or her attention in order to have a perceptual experience.
It identifies the part of the body used as a sensory organ of the #Perceiver_agentive.
It is the position of the perceiver during the act of perception.

The #Direction is used for all path-like expressions that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception, with the exception of those indicating the location of the #Perceiver_agentive (which would be marked #Location_of_perceiver).
The #State is used for predicate expressions that apply to the #Phenomenon, providing some information about the state it is in while the perceiver's attention is directed to it.
It is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the #Phenomenon.
The #Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of active perception.
The #Means indicates the tool or method the #Perceiver_agentive is using to actively perceive the #Phenomenon.
When the activity of attempting to perceive occurs.
Some action that the #Perceiver_agentive is trying to accomplish by directing their own attention.
The general location within which the act of perception takes place.
It identifies the #Duration of  time for which the Perception takes place.
An entity or state-of-affairs that the #Perceiver_agentive hopes, fears, or expects to find within the #Phenomenon.
It is the perceptual background against which the #Phenomenon is experienced by the #Perceiver_agentive.

The #Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the #Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the #Perceiver_agentive. This is similar to the #Direction, but occurs in the opposite direction and implies a movement of the #Phenomenon.

The Experiencer is the being who has a physical experience on some part of his  or her body, or internally. Often information about this frame element is incorporated as a possessive determiner into the constituent expressing the Body Part, in which case it is not tagged separately (from Body Part), as shown below: My legs <t>hurt</t>! Sometimes, however, this frame element is expressed as an External Argument in its own right:  <fex name="Experiencer">I</fex> <t>hurt</t> all over! Here, the phrase <ment>all over</ment> can be considered a kind of generalized Body Part expression, and the pronoun <ment>I</ment> expresses the Experiencer independently. With nouns and adjectives in this frame, the separate expression of the Experiencer is more common: <fex name="Experiencer">I</fex> have a <t>pain</t> in my leg.

This FE is the location on the body where the physical experience takes place, typically expressed as External Argument, often as PP complement: <fex name="Body_part">My legs</fex> are a little <t>sore</t>. I m a little <t>sore</t> <fex name="Body_part">in my legs</fex>.
The #Explanation describes the cause of the perception the body is experiencing.
Degree to which event occurs
Manner of performing an action
The #Subregion is the specific area of the #Body_part which experiences the sensation. <ex><fex name="Body_part">My leg</fex> <t>aches</t> <fex name="Subregion">under the bandage</fex>. </ex>
This FE is the being who has a perceptual experience, not necessarily on purpose. It is typically expressed as an External Argument.
This FE is the entity or phenomenon that the perceiver experiences with his or her senses. It is typically expressed as Object with verbs.

The #Body_part is the location on the #Perceiver_passive s body where the perceptual experience  takes place.  It is typically expressed in a PP. This Frame Element occurs less frequently than others, because many target words imply that a particular part of the body is involved (e.g. smelling must be done with the nose). However, the verb feel does take this Frame Element, because touch is not localized on the body the same way other modalities are. #Body_part may be expressed in other modalities when it adds specificity to what is implied by the target word in question.

This FE identifies the #Location_of_protagonist during the perceptual experience. It is typically expressed in a <ment>from</ment>-PP.
<ex>The witnesses <t>saw</t> the robbery <fex name="Location_of_protagonist">from the their car</fex>.</ex>

This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver s attention is directed during the act of perception.
<ex>The detective could easily <t>see</t> <fex name="Direction">into the house</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The detective <t>heard</t> their conversation <fex name="Direction">through the wall</fex>.</ex>

The #State indicates predicate expressions that apply to the #Phenomenon, providing some information about the state it is in while the #Perceiver_passive experiences it.

<ex>The detective <t>saw</t> the suspect <fex name="Depictive">fleeing</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Pat <t>saw</t> Kim <fex name="Depictive">naked</fex>.</ex>

#State is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>I <t>saw</t> the baker <fex name="State">baking his special peanut butter pie</fex></ex>.

<ex>I <t>overheard</t> the cat <fex name="State">in the cupboard</fex></ex>.

<ex>I <t>heard</t> fear <fex name="State">in his voice</fex></ex>.

Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of perception as such: <ment>clearly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>, etc.

<ex>Pat <t>heard</t> the shot <fex name="Manner">clearly</fex>.</ex>

The #Degree indicates the degree to which the Perception is experienced.

<ex>A nightmare creature, <fex name="Degr">barely</fex> <t>perceived</t> in the swirling dense smoke, was beating at the flames coming from its own body.</ex>
<ex>Older people <t>perceive</t> <fex name="Degr">less well</fex>.</ex>

The action whereby the Perceiver perceives.

<ex>He <t>saw</t> it <fex name="Mns">by looking out the window</fex>.</ex>
The #Time at which the #Perceiver_passive or the #Body_part perceives the #Phenomenon.
Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.

An element of the discourse that serves to contextualize the #Phenomenon, with which it is directly related (i.e. filling a frame-element role of the Phenomenon or vice-versa).  It contrasts with other contextually relevant elements that potentially (but do not actually) fill the same role.

<ex>Such a difference in note-taking accuracy can also be <t>seen</t> <fex name="Contrastive_context">for second-year pupils</fex> .</ex>

This FE signifies that the state-of-affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state-of-affairs would be expected given the state-of-affairs in the concessive clause.

A situation which pertains during the event.  This frame element is primarily used to highlight the event denoted by the target against a (potentially partially) causal or concessive background state-of-affairs.
An expression of how often the event occurs.
The general location within which the act of perception takes place.

The #Ground is the perceptual background against which the #Phenomenon is  experienced (or not experienced) by the #Perceiver_passive.  It can be distinguished from locative postnominal modifiers of the #Phenomenon in negative contexts.
<ex>Pat didn t <t>see</t> Kim  <fex name="Ground">behind the bar</fex>.</ex>
In this example, it is implied that Pat looked behind the bar and did not find Kim there.

The #Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the #Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the #Perceiver_passive.  This is similar to the #Direction, but occurs in the opposite direction and implies a movement of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>My mother <t>heard</t> the scream <fex name="Obscuring_medium">through the attic window</fex></ex>.

<ex>Her mother <t>shelved</t> the cans <fex name="Src">from the countertop</fex>.</ex>

Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal expresses the frame element #Path. In this frame, #Path expressions almost always have a <ment>via</ment>-sense.

<ex>The mail carrier <t>stuck</t> the letters <fex name="Path">through the slot</fex>.</ex>

The FE #Goal is the location where the #Theme ends up. This FE is profiled by words in this frame.  
<ex>The waiter <t>placed</t> the food <fex name="Goal">on the table</fex>.</ex>

Any expression which describes a property of the placing action which is not directly related to the trajectory of the #Theme s motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as #Manner expressions.

<ex>The chef <t>packaged</t> the food <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex> into take-out containers.</ex>
The #Degree indicates the degree to which the placing occurs.

This FE identifies any #Depictive phrase describing the #Agent or #Theme of the placing.


<ex>I <t>put</t> the dishes into the cabinet <fex name="Depictive">unwashed</fex>.</ex>

The #Means is an act whereby the #Agent achieves the placing of the #Theme.

<ex>The Egyptians <t>set</t> it in place <fex name="Mns">by simple levering</fex>.</ex>
This FE identifies the #Result of the Placing.

The #Duration is the amount of time for which the #Theme is to stay in the #Goal location.

<ex>If you <t>place</t> it there <fex name="Duration">for extended periods</fex>, your monitor may suffer permanent damage.</ex>
The #Place indicates where the Placing occurs.
The #Explanation indicates why the Placing occurs.

#Time is when the Placing occurs

<ex>Smithers had <t>placed</t> the table there <fex name="Time">the night before</fex>.</ex>

The #Area is the setting into which the #Theme is placed.


<ex>She emptied a wash basket full of towels and <t>deposited</t> them <fex name="Area">around the house</fex>.</ex>

This is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.

<ex>Pat <t>lay</t> the trophy on the mantle <fex name="Cotheme">along with her portrait</fex>. </ex>

This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.  

<ex>I <t>plunged</t> my hands <fex name="Distance">wrist deep</fex> in the fragrant herbs and rubbed the dry fragments between the fingertips</ex>

The #Speed is the rate at which Placing occurs.

<ex>Smithers <t>put</t> his book down <fex name="Speed">quickly</fex> and turned around.</ex>
<ex>Grass , which is sown with clover , provides rich pasture for cattle in summer and the clover is <fex name="Cause">another plant which</fex> <t>puts</t> nitrogen into the soil .</ex>

An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by perform the placing action.

<ex>They <t>put</t> the vase in a box <fex name="Purpose">to protect it during transportation</fex>.</ex>

This extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.
The #Agent is the actor who instigates the filling.

The #Theme is the physical object or substance which changes location. <ex><fex name="Agent">Rev. Lovejoy</fex> <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex> <t>brushed</t> <fex name="Goal">the rolls</fex> <fex name="Theme">with butter</fex>. </ex>
The #Source is the location where the #Theme begins its motion. <ex><fex name="Agent">Josef</fex> <t>filled</t> <fex name="Goal">his flask</fex> <fex name="Source">from the whiskey bottle</fex>.</ex>
The #Path is the trajectory of the motion.  <ex><fex name="Agent">Jess</fex> <t>filled</t> <fex name="Goal">the container</fex> <fex name="Path">through a small hole in the lid</fex>.</ex>
The #Goal is the area or container being filled. Goal is generally the NP Object in this frame.
#Degree to which the filling occurs.

Depictive phrase describing the #Agent of the filling action.
<ex><fex name="Dep">Naked</fex>, <fex name="Agent">Margeret</fex> <t>filled</t> <fex name="Goal">the tub</fex>.</ex>
The #Manner in which the filling occurs.
The #Means by which the filling occurs.
The #Result is the resultant state after the filling has occurred.

The <fex name="Explanation">Explanation</fex> for which the #Agent fills or covers the #Goal. <ex><fex name="Agent">Samantha</fex> <t>covered</t> <fex name="Goal">the table</fex> <fex name="Theme">with flowers</fex> <fex name="Explanation">because she was bored</fex>.</ex>
When the filling occurs.
Where the filling takes place.
The #Instrument with which the filling occurs.

The #Subregion is the smaller area of the #Goal where the filling or covering occurs. <ex><fex name="Agent">Mrs. Bouvier</fex> <t>anointed</t> <fex name="Goal">the children</fex> <fex name="Theme">with oil</fex> <fex name="Subregion">on the forehead</fex>.</ex>
An event which brings about the filling of the #Goal.
The #Theme is the entity that traverses the trajectory.
The #Source profiles the region where the #Theme has travelled from (i.e. the region where the #Theme s movement began).
The #Path is the region upon which the #Theme travels. It is any region in between the #Source (where the #Theme departed from) and the #Goal (where the theme will eventually arrive).
The #Goal profiles the region at the end of the #Theme s #Path. This is the region where the #Theme movement stops.
The #Descriptor provides additional information that characterizes one or more of the aspects of the trajectory: the #Source, the #Path, or the #Goal.
Person or group seeking information to prepare the #Touristic_activity.
Programming made by the #Tourist to organize entertainment activities in which she will engage during the trip. It also includes procedures such as obtaining documents and purchasing airline tickets.
Where the #Tourist plans the #Touristic_activity
Where the #Tourist intends to arrive.
Any specific action that #Tourist wants to accomplish with the #Touristic_activity, such as entertainment and leisure, for example.

Why the #Tourist decides to plan the #Touristic_activity.
Individual or group who plans the #Touristic_activity together with the #Tourist.
Actions or means that enable the planning of the #Touristic_activity.
How the #Tourist plans the #Touristic_activity.
When the planning of the #Touristic_activity happens.
The duration of the planning process.

For what length of time the #Agent plants the #Theme.
The #Manner in which the #Agent plants the #Theme.

The failure or success of an attempt.
The greater geographic location where the #Agent plants the #Theme.
When the planting event occurs.
An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by planting the <fex name="Theme">Theme</fex>.
How often the #Agent cultivates the #Theme.
This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
The #Ground is the background or context to which the #Agent pays attention in his agricultural efforts.
Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
The #Depictive describes a participant in the Planting action.
The #Co-participant is an entity that participates in the planting event in a coordinated way with another participant.
The #Explanation denotes a proposition from which the planting event logically follows.
The living organism belonging to the kingdom Plantae.
A quality of the #Plant.
A characterization of the #Plant not covered by another frame element.
The location from which the #Plant originated.

The #Question is an open proposition the answer to which is under dispute.

<ex>The <t>issue</t> is <fex name="que">who will have control of Iraq s rich oil resources</fex>.</ex>
The #Group are the persons or organizations who have different points of view on the #Question.

The Frame Element #Status is used for expressions that indicate the relative importance of a #Question with respect to the other topics that are disputed.


<ex>The <fex name="sta">main</fex> <t>issue</t> is where the revenue is coming in from.</ex>

A characterization of the #Question, often with regard to its complexity or whether it has been resolved or not.

<ex>Two <fex name="des">unresolved</fex> <t>issues</t> fuel speculation that he might not receive a fair trial.</ex>

The area of human experience which the #Question concerns.

<ex>The third sector of the Master Plan explores the important <fex name="dom">economic</fex> <t>issue</t> of tourism.</ex>

A state or event within which the #Question arises as a problem for the #Group.

<ex>The <t>question</t> <fex name="Context">in Argentina s case</fex> is what exchange rate is appropriate now.</ex>

The time interval during which the #Question exists as a problem for the #Group.

<ex>The <t>issue</t> <fex name="tim">at the time</fex> was whether or not to relocate the Rennes-based part of
the team to California.</ex>

An individual or individuals from whose point of view the #Question is a point of dispute within the #Group.

<ex><fex name="Viewpoint">For Elijah</fex>, the <t>issue</t> was between two altars: worship of God and worship of BaaI.</ex>
Individual or group that resorts to the #Police at some #Place.
It refers to buildings, institutions and individuals who represent the forces of public security of some #Place.
Geographic location visited by the #Tourist, in which is the #Police.
Some specific action that is made possible by the existence of the #Police.
Specific type of the #Police.

The person or object that is being evaluated for its desirability.

<ex>I heard he s a <t>cool</t> <fex name="Evaluee">guy</fex>.</ex>

A description of how desirable the #Evaluee is.

<ex>Hanna Montana is <fex name="Degree">really</fex> <t>cool</t>.</ex>

The person who decides how desirable the #Evaluee is.

<ex>Knitting is <t>cool</t> <fex name="Judge">to your grandma</fex>.</ex>

The time when the Evaluee is judged to be desirable.

<ex>Go-go boots were <t>popular</t> <fex name="Time">in the 60s</fex>.</ex>

The geographic area where an #Evaluee is considered desirable.

<ex>Electronica is <t>all the rage</t> <fex name="Place">in Europe</fex>.</ex>
The #Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that they are a particular individual or type of #Simulated_entity.

Any description of the posing event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>).

An intentional action performed by the #Agent that contributes to the his or her being perceived as a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>Some activists <t>posed</t> as delegates <fex name="Means">by wearing suits</fex>.</ex>
The type or individual that the #Agent gives the impression of being.

An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by posing as a particular (kind of) #Simulated_entity.

<ex>He <t>posed</t> as a Hurricane Katrina evacuee <fex name="pur">in order to get food, clothing and money from Good Samaritans in Shreveport</fex>.</ex>

The #Explanation denotes a proposition from which the posing event logically follows. 

<ex>They <t>posed</t> as patients <fex name="exp">because the paper s previous attempt at a formal survey met strong resistance</fex>.</ex>
The location where the #Agent gives the impression that they are the #Simulated_entity.
The time at which the #Agent impersonates the #Simulated_entity.

Degree to which event occurs

This FE marks expressions that indicate an event or state that is predicted to occur or hold at a future time. A single #Eventuality may be expressed discontinuously in a raising construction. This FE is also used for expressions that denote entities with variable attributes about which the #Speaker makes predictions. 

<ex> <t>Predicting</t> <fex name="Eventuality">the weather</fex> is risky business.</ex>
<ex><t>Predicting</t> <fex name="Eventuality">the numbers of disabled elderly</fex> into the future is problematic as it depends upon the projection forward of current patterns of disability .</ex>
<ex> Wang<t>forecasts</t> <fex name="Eventuality">a 9 per cent increase in state revenue</fex></ex>
<ex> <fex name="Eventuality">The European market</fex> is  <t>forecast</t> <fex name="Eventuality">to grow 18.1%</fex>.</ex>

The #Place where the #Speaker makes the prediction.

<ex> Greenman <t>predicted</t> <fex name="Place">at a small dinner party</fex> that the market would rise again.</ex>

#Manner marks any description of the #Speaker s prediction making  that is not covered by more specific FEs.

<ex>One commentator <fex name="Manner">rashly</fex> <t>predicted</t> that the fad for adaptions wouldn t last much longer</ex>

Note that this FE does not cover modifiers such as  correctly  or  accurately .

This frame element marks expressions that indicate forwhat future time an #Eventuality is predicted.

This FE describes a characteristic of the #Eventuality.

The #Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the #Speaker to transmit the prediction.

An act whereby the #Speaker makes a prediction.

<ex>How do you <t>reassure</t> them <fex name="Msg">that you really like them</fex>?  </ex></def-root>

Situation is the problem plaguing the #Experiencer, about which the #Speaker  reassures them.

<ex>The policeman <t>reassured</t> him <fex name="Situation">about the safety of the neighborhood</fex>. </ex>
The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.

The amount of reassurance given.
<ex>He never really <fex name="Amount_of_reassurance">fully</fex> <t>reassured</t> me about the situation.</ex>
The #Depictive describes the state of the #Speaker.

The #Means by which the #Speaker reassures the #Experiencer.

<ex>Him mother reassured him <fex name="Mns">with a pat on the back</fex>.</ex>

The time at which the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="tim">before the month was out</fex>.</ex>

The system whereby the #Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

The state of the #Recipient or #Theme during the action.

<ex>I didn t expect to <t>receive</t> this steak <fex name="dep">absolutely raw</fex>!</ex>

The character of the #Recipient which saliently affects the action, or a general description of the action itself.

<ex>He <fex name="man">slyly</fex> <t>received</t> the package without signing for it.</ex>

A good given or a service rendered by the #Recipient, in return for wich the #Theme is given to the #Recipient by the #Donor.

$100 <fex name="cou">for her suggestion to create an electronic employee evaluation form</fex>. </ex>
When the #Theme is received physically by the #Recipient from the #Donor, the #Path of motion of the #Theme  may be specified.

What the #Recipient intends to do with the #Theme.

<ex>James Madison Memorial High School <t>received</t> $1,000 <fex name="pot">to purchase instruments for school orchestra and band programs</fex>. </ex>

A role that the #Theme is intended to fill by the #Donor.

<ex>He <t>received</t> the book <fex name="rol">as a gift</fex> from his uncle.</ex>
The person who acts to come into possession of the #Theme.
The person who gives the #Theme to the #Recipient.
The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the #Donor and ending up with the #Recipient.

The aim of the #Recipient, which they believe will be accomplished by accepting the #Theme.
